events.agenda: Doors open 6:30pm for a 6:45pm start. Three back-to-back presentations.
events.event_description: This event is open to all folks working with data -- while we'll predominantly focus on people's experience with dbt, we're also very interested in broader topics related to analytics engineering, such as data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.location: Bowery Farming HQ, 151 W 26th St 12th floor, New York, NY
events.raw_text:
Thu, Feb 27, 2020 · 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM EST

Bowery Farming HQ, 151 W 26th St, New York, NY

Bowery Farming Data Team - Migrating 387 dbt models from Redshift to Snowflake.
Danielle Leong - How to hire an analytics engineer: a candidate's perspective.
Kenny Ning (Better.com) - Using dbt + Machine Learning to predict customer conversions.

talks.description: Lessons from Bowery Farming's migration of 387 dbt models from Redshift to Snowflake, including timeline, unexpected challenges, and tooling built to ease the process.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Migrating 387 dbt models from Redshift to Snowflake
The Bowery Farming Data Team
talks.title: Migrating 387 dbt models from Redshift to Snowflake

talks.description: A candidate's perspective on the hiring process for an emerging role like analytics engineer, including where to find and post roles and what makes interviews effective.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
How to hire an analytics engineer: a candidate’s perspective
Danielle Leong
talks.title: How to hire an analytics engineer: a candidate's perspective

talks.description: How Better.com built a 'full-stack data science' model using dbt and machine learning to predict which mortgage applicants would go on to fund with the company.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Using dbt + Machine Learning to predict customer conversions
Kenny Ning, Data Engineer
Better.com
talks.title: Using dbt + Machine Learning to predict customer conversions

additional_metadata.venue_host: Warby Parker HQ and Showroom, New York

events.agenda: Doors open 6pm for a 6:30pm start. Three back-to-back presentations.
events.event_description: This event is hosted by Warby Parker and open to all folks working with data -- while we'll predominantly focus on people's experience with dbt, we're also very interested in broader topics related to analytics engineering, such as data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.location: Warby Parker HQ and Showroom, 233 Spring St 6th Floor E, New York, NY
events.raw_text:
Thu, Oct 17, 2019 · 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM EDT

Warby Parker HQ, 233 Spring St, New York, NY

Sean McIntyre (Warby Parker) - inner workings of dbt.
Andie DeLeón (P.volve) - building a 'unified users' model.
Joe Naso (pymetrics) - pitfalls of a first data warehouse rollout.

talks.description: A look under the hood at how dbt works, how anyone can extend dbt by writing code in their own project, and how Warby Parker has extended and contributed dbt functionality back to the community.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Sean McIntytre, Lead Data Engineer
Warby Parker
Ever wondered how dbt actually works?
talks.title: Under the hood: how dbt actually works

talks.description: How P.volve built a 'unified users' model to stitch together users across multiple SaaS tools and identifiers (user_ids, emails, tracking cookies) into a single view of the customer.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Andie DeLeón, Data & Analytics
P.volve
You may have heard the term "single view of the customer" before.
talks.title: Building a Unified Users Model

talks.description: The pitfalls and mistakes pymetrics made rolling out its first data warehouse, which quickly became the primary data source for client-facing products.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Joe Naso, Technical Product Manager
pymetrics
You've all heard the talks about how wonderful things are after the implementation of new technology
talks.title: Lessons from Our First Data Warehouse Rollout

events.agenda: Three back-to-back presentations from Rent the Runway, Lyft Bikes & Scooters, and Casper.
events.event_description: This event is hosted by Casper and open to all folks working with data -- while we'll predominantly focus on people's experience with dbt, we're also very interested in broader topics that apply to modern data teams, such as data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.location: 230 Park Ave S, New York, NY
events.raw_text:
Wed, Jul 31, 2019 · 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM EDT

230 Park Ave S, New York, NY

Danielle Dalton (Rent the Runway) - tracking customer address changes with dbt snapshots.
Luc Alvarado (Lyft Bikes & Scooters) - designing accessible data models.
Jason Gluck & Karishma Pradhan (Casper) - engineer/analyst collaboration.

talks.description: How Rent the Runway solved the problem of tracking customer address changes for a subscription ecommerce business using dbt snapshots.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Danielle Dalton, Software Engineer
Rent the Runway
Tracking customer address changes is a problem for any ecommerce company...Learn how Rent the Runway is solving this problem with dbt snapshots.
talks.title: Tracking Customer Address Changes with dbt Snapshots

talks.description: How Lyft Bikes & Scooters designed data models to be accessible for business users amid rapid growth and a recent acquisition adding to data complexity.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Luc Alvarado, Data Scientist
Lyft Bikes & Scooters
Designing data models to be accessible for business users is a critical component of a data-driven organization.
talks.title: Designing Accessible Data Models

talks.description: How data engineers and data analysts at Casper collaborate to deploy new data models, highlighting the hallmark relationships of successful data teams.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Jason Gluck, Data Engineer; Karishma Pradhan, Data Analyst
Casper
COLLABORATION!!! Good data engineer and data analyst relationships are the hallmark of successful data teams.
talks.title: Engineer and Analyst Collaboration at Casper
